agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands courier location never be shown due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by technical design alone — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted by the company to transport packages from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The GPS coordinates of a courier updated at frequent intervals (typically every few seconds) during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to deliver a specific package to a customer."},{"term":"Customer visibility","definition":"The degree to which delivery tracking information is exposed to the end customer receiving a package."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is shared — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to zone-level approximation (e.g., '2 stops away') to no location at all."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The operational metric measuring the company's ability to keep couriers employed/contracted over time, directly impacted by working conditions and perceived surveillance."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived value that can provide delivery transparency without exposing raw location data."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and exact GPS is shown","expected_behavior":"Customer may perceive courier as idle or lost, generating support tickets. Courier feels monitored during legitimate breaks. This scenario amplifies both teams' concerns simultaneously."},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer's home but delivering to a neighbor first","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS shows courier 'right there' but not arriving, causing frustration and calls. A zone-based approach ('1 stop away') sets better expectations."},{"scenario":"Courier delivers in a high-crime area and location is exposed","expected_behavior":"Safety risk to courier if location data is intercepted or shared. This is a physical safety invariant that transcends either team's preference."},{"scenario":"Courier is multi-dropping (batch deliveries) and route is visible","expected_behavior":"Customer sees courier driving away from them before coming back — confusing with exact GPS, but perfectly explainable with 'You are stop 3 of 5' messaging."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or shares courier's exact location on social media","expected_behavior":"Privacy violation and potential harassment vector for courier. Once exact coordinates are shown, the company loses control of that data."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"If drivers leave due to surveillance concerns, recruitment and training costs increase significantly (industry average: $3,000–$5,000 per driver replacement). Conversely, Customer Success claims tracking transparency drives customer acquisition and retention — lost transparency could reduce conversion. The financial calculus depends on which cost is larger: driver churn or customer churn. This data has not been provided.","operational":"Driver shortage from retention problems directly degrades delivery capacity, SLAs, and service quality. A fleet that cannot retain drivers cannot deliver packages regardless of how good the customer-facing tracking is. Operational risk from the Operations team's concern is arguably more existential.","user":"Customers want predictability and confidence their package will arrive. Research across the logistics industry (Amazon, UberEats, DoorDash) shows that ETA countdown and status updates ('Your courier is 2 stops away') deliver nearly equivalent customer satisfaction to raw GPS without the privacy cost. The Customer Success team may be conflating 'transparency' with 'exact GPS coordinates' when the actual customer need is delivery confidence.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping either requirement as stated creates material business harm. Showing exact GPS risks driver attrition, privacy complaints, and potential GDPR/privacy regulation exposure. Hiding all location info risks customer dissatisfaction and competitive disadvantage. Additionally, building the feature before resolving the conflict risks expensive rework.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"CRITICAL — Exact GPS exposure creates perceived surveillance, reduces job satisfaction, raises personal safety concerns, and is cited as a direct cause of attrition. Their labor makes the business possible."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"HIGH — Want delivery confidence and predictability. Actual need may be met by ETA/status rather than raw coordinates, but removing all visibility would be a regression if competitors offer it."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"HIGH — Their KPIs around NPS and customer satisfaction are tied to tracking transparency. Need to understand that 'transparency' has a spectrum of implementations."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"HIGH — Responsible for fleet capacity and driver retention. A shrinking driver pool is an existential operational threat."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"MEDIUM — Sharing employee/contractor real-time GPS with third parties has privacy regulation implications (GDPR, CCPA) that neither team has addressed."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"HIGH — Must arbitrate this conflict. This is a strategic business decision, not a feature decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the two authoritative stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements with no resolution path provided. There is no executive decision, no compromise framework, and no data shared to evaluate the relative business impact of each position. Additionally, no legal/privacy review has been referenced. I cannot define correct acceptance criteria when the definition of 'correct' is actively contested by the business itself.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine business policy contradiction, not a technical misunderstanding","Courier safety and privacy are legitimate domain constraints in logistics","Customer delivery confidence can be achieved through multiple UX patterns beyond raw GPS","Driver retention is an existential operational concern in the courier industry"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder team's priority should take precedence — this is an executive decision","Whether a compromise (e.g., zone-based tracking, ETA-only) would satisfy Customer Success","The actual financial impact of driver churn vs. customer churn — no data provided","Privacy/legal obligations in the company's operating jurisdictions","Whether drivers have contractual or consent-based agreements regarding location sharing"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholders' positions","No executive decision or policy has already been made on this matter","The company operates in jurisdictions where employee/contractor location privacy is regulated","There exist intermediate solutions between 'exact GPS' and 'no location at all'"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location NEVER be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two teams with equal apparent authority. This cannot be resolved at the feature level — it requires executive arbitration on business strategy and values.","affected_stakeholders":["Couriers/Drivers","Customers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations requirement, risks driver attrition)","B: Show no location (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success requirement, risks customer dissatisfaction)","C: Compromise — show approximate location or zone-based tracking (e.g., 'nearby', '2 stops away', ETA countdown) with driver consent. This is the industry-standard pattern used by major logistics companies.","D: Make it driver-opt-in — couriers choose whether to share location, customers see ETA-only as baseline"],"recommendation":"Option C or D. The logistics industry has largely converged on abstracted location sharing (zone, ETA, status) rather than raw GPS. This preserves customer confidence without creating surveillance dynamics. However, I CANNOT set acceptance criteria until an executive decision is made. Recommend escalating to VP-level with data on driver churn cost vs. customer satisfaction metrics for GPS vs. ETA-based tracking."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No legal or privacy review has been conducted on sharing contractor/employee real-time GPS coordinates with third parties. In GDPR and CCPA jurisdictions, this may require explicit driver consent and could be legally impermissible without it, which would make the Customer Success requirement not just operationally harmful but unlawful.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage legal counsel to determine regulatory constraints before any design work","Treat legal constraints as hard boundaries that narrow the solution space"],"recommendation":"Engage legal immediately. Regulatory constraints may resolve this conflict by eliminating Option A entirely."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot accept 'show exact GPS coordinates at all times' as a requirement","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Operations team's equally-weighted requirement that courier location must never be shown. Implementing either side without executive resolution would create organizational conflict and likely require rework."},{"violation":"Cannot accept 'never show courier location' as a requirement","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Customer Success team's equally-weighted requirement for GPS transparency. Both requirements as stated are rejected until the contradiction is resolved by appropriate business authority."},{"violation":"No privacy impact assessment has been conducted","business_rule_broken":"Sharing real-time GPS coordinates of workers with external parties is a privacy-sensitive operation that requires legal review before any acceptance criteria can be defined. This is a hard prerequisite."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands courier location never be shown due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by technical design alone — i
